questions.question: Which feature engineering technique involves creating new features that capture the interaction between existing features?
questions.explanation: Polynomial transformation is a technique used to model non-linear relationships by creating new features that are polynomial combinations of existing features.

questions.question: Which dimensionality reduction technique is based on finding the directions of maximum variance in the data?
questions.explanation: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is a technique used to reduce the dimensionality of data by selecting the directions of maximum variance.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of feature selection?
options: A) To reduce noise in the data
B) To select the most informative features
C) To reduce the number of features
questions.explanation: Feature selection is the process of selecting a subset of the most informative features from the data, which can improve model performance and interpretability.

questions.question: Which dimensionality reduction technique is based on the idea that similar data points should be mapped to the same point?
questions.explanation: Local Linear Embedding (LLE) is a technique used to reduce the dimensionality of data by preserving the local structure of the data.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using Principal Component Analysis (PCA) over other dimensionality reduction techniques?
options: A) It is faster to compute
C) It is more robust to noise
questions.explanation: PCA is a widely used dimensionality reduction technique because it is interpretable, fast to compute, and robust to noise.
